fields.notes:
Original production schedule:
11:30 AM load-in and microphone setup.
12:30 PM MPA preparation.
1:45–4:00 PM LACO rehearsal.
4:00–5:00 PM crew break.
Jerome arrives at 5:00 PM.
House opens at 5:30 PM.
LACO performs 6:00–6:30 PM.
MPA / Cut Piece performs 6:30–7:30 PM.
Cleanup and load-out begins at 7:45 PM.

Nathaniel's updated walkthrough:
• Record at true 24.00 fps.
• Jerome should stay mobile on a monopod rather than work from a fixed tripod.
• Jerome will use the Panasonic S5IIX with the Samyang 35–150mm because parts of the venue are low light.
• Use the large Neewer monitor.
• Nathaniel will provide production comms so the camera team can communicate.
• Coverage includes audience shots and audience movement/interaction.
• For Cut Piece, preserve the performer and 4×4 platform while anticipating audience approaches and reactions.
• Jerome was busy during the verbal walkthrough, so exact starting position, movement boundaries, synchronization, profile, codec, audio, and media handoff should be reconfirmed at the 5:00 PM briefing.
fields.inventoryNotes:
CONFIRMED PRIMARY PACKAGE
• Panasonic S5IIX body
• Samyang 35–150mm f/2–2.8 L-mount
• Monopod with secure plate/head
• Large Neewer monitor
• Monitor mounting arm and correct HDMI cable
• Monitor batteries / power solution
• Headphones
• Production comms supplied by Nathaniel
• Primary and backup media cards
• Primary and backup S5IIX batteries
• Chargers
• Lens cloth / blower
• Gaffer tape
• Multi-tool
• Water and snack

VERIFY BEFORE LEAVING
• Monopod plate is mounted and tightened.
• Monitor arm will not twist while moving.
• HDMI cable has strain relief and does not interfere with monopod movement.
• Monitor brightness is reduced after house opens.
• Comms earpiece does not interfere with headphones or camera operation.
• 24.00 fps is selected—not 23.98.
• Shutter is matched to Nathaniel's instruction; likely 1/48 or 180° once confirmed.
• Lens balance and monopod handling are comfortable with the 35–150mm.
• Cards and batteries cover continuous or near-continuous recording if requested.
fields.customShots:
MOBILE CAMERA PRIORITIES — JEROME
1. Audience arrival and room atmosphere before the performance.
2. Audience listening reactions during the LACO sextet.
3. Clean musician singles, hands, bows, fingering, and two-shots when Nathaniel calls for them.
4. Transition coverage as the stage changes for Cut Piece.
5. Full-body performer coverage with the entire 4×4 platform protected.
6. Audience members leaving their seats and approaching the platform.
7. The full approach path—not only the interaction at the platform.
8. Hands, scissors/object interaction, and clothing detail when appropriate.
9. Performer facial reactions throughout the progression.
10. Audience hesitation, anticipation, surprise, and emotional reactions.
11. Return movement from platform back toward the audience when useful.
12. Complete ending, final interaction, performer reaction, applause, and a long ending hold.

OPERATING RULE
Stay mobile on the monopod, but do not create unnecessary movement. Follow comms, hold each usable composition long enough to edit, avoid blocking audience sightlines, and verify the record indicator after every reposition.
fields.questions:
CONFIRMED
• Frame rate: 24.00 fps.
• Support: monopod so Jerome can stay mobile.
• Camera: Panasonic S5IIX.
• Lens: Samyang 35–150mm f/2–2.8.
• Monitor: large Neewer monitor.
• Communication: production comms will be supplied for crew communication.
• Coverage includes audience activity and audience interaction during Cut Piece.

STILL CONFIRM WITH NATHANIEL
1. What resolution and recording codec should I match?
2. What picture profile should I match: Standard / Rec.709, V-Log, or another profile?
3. How are the cameras being synchronized?
4. Does my camera need scratch audio, a board feed, or no usable audio?
5. Am I expected to roll continuously, selectively, or use a mixed approach?
6. Who takes possession of my media at wrap?
7. What is my exact starting camera position and movement boundary?
8. During Cut Piece, which side is my primary audience approach lane?
9. Are there participant-privacy or framing restrictions?
10. Where should I park and which entrance should I use?
11. Is the expected release still approximately 8:15 PM?
fields.customRisks: Mobile monopod operation with a heavy 35–150mm and large monitor may fatigue the operator and increase cable/monitor-arm risk. Keep the rig balanced, protect audience sightlines, and avoid moving during critical endings. Confirm privacy and framing boundaries for audience participation in Cut Piece.
